content.rendered:
This page contains all the Finnish grammar subjects that Uusi kielemme currently covers. There are separate sections on verbs, nouns, constructions, phonetics and the grammar of spoken language.

Please note that these Finnish grammar topics are not listed in the order you should study them. While it is generally true that the column on the left contains the most basic subjects, this does not always hold true.

The idea of this page is that it works as a reference material for Finnish grammar: whenever you have questions about a subject, you can look up more information on this page. Within one article, there can be information fit for beginners as well as more complicated information for advanced learners.

Table of Contents
  1. Grammar related to verbs
  2. Grammar related to nouns
  3. Grammar related to sentence constructions
  4. Spoken language grammar
  5. Phonology
  6. Etymology
The Conjugation of Verbs

THE CONJUGATION OF VERBS

  • The present tense
    • The Finnish verbtypes
  • The past tenses
    • The imperfect tense
      • Lentää > lensin
      • Auttaa > autoin
    • The negative imperfect
    • The perfect tense
    • The plusquamperfect
    • Overview of past tenses
  • The future tense
    • Ways to express intent
  • The passive
    • The present passive
    • Negative present passive
    • The passive imperfect
    • The passive perfect tense
    • The passive conditional
    • Overview of the passives
    • Spoken language passive
  • The conditional mood
    • The conditional
    • The perfect conditional
    • The passive conditional
  • The imperative mood
    • The imperative
    • Älä! Negative imperative
    • Kapteeni käskee (singular)
    • Kapteeni käskee (plural)
    • Other imperative uses
  • Consonant gradation
    • Verbtype 1
    • Verbtype 3
    • Verbtype 4
    • Verbtype 6
  • The infinitives
    • The 5 Finnish infinitives
    • The first infinitive
    • The second infinitive
    • The third infinitive
    • The fourth infinitive
  • The participles
    • Overview
    • Active present participle (-VA)
    • Passive present participle (-TAVA)
    • Active past participle (-NUT)
    • Passive past participle (-TU)
    • Agent participle (-mA)
  • Making verbs negative
  • Compound verbs
  • The potential mood
  • Verbtype 1
  • Verbtype 2 peculiarity
  • Finnish verb stems
The Inflection of Nouns

THE INFLECTION OF NOUNS

  • The partitive case
  • The genitive case
  • The location cases
    • Missä mistä mihin
    • The inessive (-ssa)
    • The elative (-sta)
    • The illative
    • The adessive (-lla)
    • The ablative (-lta)
    • The allative (-lle)
    • Hyllyllä or hyllyssä?
  • Consonant Gradation
    • Wordtype A – KPT
    • Wordtype B – KPT
  • Degrees of comparison
    • Comparative of adjectives
    • Partitive in comparatives
    • Superlative of adjectives
    • Inflecting comparisons
    • Comparing adverbs
    • Inflecting comparative adverbs
  • The T-plural
  • The plural partitive
    • The plural partitive
    • Plural of long words
    • Plural partitive vs T-plural
    • List: Kissa-words
    • List: Koira-words
  • The plural genitive
    • The plural genitive
    • Plural of long words
    • Using the plural genitive
    • Possessive suffixes
  • The plural location cases
    • The plural location cases
    • The plural illative
    • The plural translative
  • Examples of the plural cases
  • Rarer cases
    • The accusative case
    • Accusative total objects
    • The translative (-ksi)
    • The essive (-na)
    • The abessive (-tta)
    • The comitative (-ine)
    • The instructive (-in)
  • “Here” and “there”
    • Overview
    • Tässä tästä tähän
    • Täällä täältä tänne
    • Tuossa tuosta tuohon
    • Tuolla tuolta tuonne
    • Siinä siitä siihen
  • The inflection of…
    • Expressions of time
    • Place names
    • Words ending in a consonant
    • Us-Ukse- vs Us-Ude- words
    • Words ending in -is
    • Old/New words ending in -i
    • Inflection of words like UUSI
    • Inflection of words like OVI
    • Inflection of words like PIENI
    • Inflection of words like TOIMI
  • Special inflection cases
    • Inflection of numbers
    • Inflection of ordinal numbers
    • Inflection of foreign names
    • Inflection of French names
    • Indeclinable words
    • Incongruent phrases
  • Old cases
    • Prolative: puhelimitse vesitse
    • Lative: alas ulos ylös
Finnish Sentence Constructions

SYNTAX: Sentence Constructions

Finnish Sentence Types

  • Overview of sentence types
  • Possession (having something)
  • Necessity sentences (täytyy)
  • Täytyy-sentence types
  • Syntax of täytyy-sentences
  • Conjugation of täytyy-sentences
  • On mentävä -sentences
  • Existential sentences
  • Word order in existential sentences
  • Generic sentences
  • Change and result sentences
  • Tuloslause vs. muutoslause
  • Subordinate clauses
  • Relative pronoun joka
  • Sentence types with the verb
    in the third person singular

Constructions

  • [olla + -lla] (olla kahvilla)
  • [olla + -ssa] (olla piilossa)
  • [olla + -uksissaan]
  • [x:llani, x:llaan] (pahoillaan)
  • [olla + -ltaan] (ammatiltaan)
  • [olla + -ttavissa] (tavattavissa)

Clitics – Liitepartikkelit

  • The order of Finnish suffixes
  • -han/-hän
  • -kin/-kaan/-kään
  • -pa/-pä

Rections

  • What are rections?
  • Verb + noun rections
    • Partitive verbs
    • So-called partitive verbs
    • Käydä + missä
    • Location case verb rections
    • Translative verb rections
    • Perceptional verbs
    • Keneltä kenestä keneen
  • Verb + verb rections
    • Infinitive verb rections
    • Third infinitive verb rections
    • Fourth infinitive verb rections
  • Noun rections
    • Adjective rections
    • Noun + noun rections
    • Noun + verb rections
  • Verb rections with että
  • Timespans: mistä mihin
  • Use of infinitives and participles

Grammatical Functions

  • Object & Complement
    • The object
    • Luen kirjaa/kirjan/kirjat
    • Syön leipää/pullan/nakkeja
    • Tarvitsen rakkautta
    • Advanced object: overview
    • Total object verbs
    • The complement
    • Mass nouns
    • Complications
    • Abstract nouns
  • The subject

Advanced

  • Lauseenvastikkeet
    • Finaalirakenne
    • Modaalirakenne
    • Temporaalirakenne
    • Referatiivirakenne
  • Colorative verb chains
  • Reduplication phrases
  • Reduplication words
  • Relative pronouns JOKA and MIKÄ
Other Areas of Finnish

Other Areas of Finnish

Spoken language

  • Mä mää mie – Pronouns
  • Tää toi se – Taivutus
  • Ne puhuu puhekieltä
  • Menen nukkuun – Third infinitive
  • Mun tarttee syödä
  • Verbs in spoken language
  • Spoken language passive
  • Spoken language questions
  • Spoken language numbers
  • Spoken language pronunciation
  • Syntax of spoken Finnish
  • South Ostrobothnian dialect
  • Overview

Morphology

  • What is derivation?
  • Finnish derivational elements

Phonology

  • Vowel harmony
  • Vowel harmony exceptions
  • Consonant gradation
  • Tilli tili tiili tyyli tuuli
  • Banaani vs panaani
  • Vanha vs wanha
  • Pöydällä vs pöyrällä
  • Kolome kylymää kahavia
  • Siivota → siipoan?
  • Politiikka vs poliitikko
  • Menep pois

Spelling

  • The use of capital letters
  • Comma rules in sentences
  • Comma rules in phrases

Etymology

  • Swedish loanverbs -ATA
  • English loanverbs -ATA
  • Baltic and Germanic loanwords

Duolingo

  • Introduction to the partitive
  • Tässä täällä tuossa tuolla

Specific consonant gradation

  • RK/LK – RJ/LJ
  • K – disappears
